target.title: fix(agent): abort accepted gateway runs on signal
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent` could keep waiting after an external `SIGTERM` or `SIGINT` while a Gateway-backed agent run had already been accepted server-side. For cron, systemd, and other supervisors, that meant the CLI process could be interrupted without asking the Gateway to cancel the in-flight run it had just started.

## Change and value

This PR propagates caller abort signals through the Gateway client path, records the accepted run id while the CLI waits for the final response, and has `openclaw agent` send `chat.abort` before exiting on `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT`. The abort request reuses the same Gateway identity as the original agent run, including the backend/admin authority used for `--model` override runs, so cleanup remains authorized for both default and model override execution.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators running `openclaw agent` through cron, systemd, shell `timeout`, CI jobs, or any wrapper that may send `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT` while a Gateway-backed run is active.
- Affected: Gateway client callers that need abort propagation and accepted-run visibility while waiting for a final response.
- Not changed: Gateway cron's existing isolated-run watchdogs, process-supervisor hard-kill behavior, provider cancellation semantics after a process has already been killed, or broader launcher/orphan cleanup.

## Why now

Issue #71710 is still current on `main`: server-side accepted-run abort support exists, but the one-shot agent CLI did not bridge process signals into the Gateway request or follow up with `chat.abort` for the accepted run. This PR closes that caller-side lifecycle gap without adding a public knob.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #71710
- Related #71214
- Related #77481
- Related #82403
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Implementation

- `GatewayClient.request` now accepts an optional caller `AbortSignal` and an `onAccepted` callback for callers that wait for accepted Gateway runs.
- `callGateway` forwards the caller signal and accepted-run callback into `GatewayClient`.
- `agent-via-gateway` installs `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT` listeners around Gateway-backed and local embedded CLI runs, aborts the waiting request, and sends `chat.abort` for the accepted run id and resolved session key.
- The follow-up `chat.abort` uses the same Gateway identity as the original agent request. Default runs keep CLI identity; `--model` override runs keep backend/admin authority.
- Abort errors short-circuit before embedded Gateway fallback classification, so a user-requested abort cannot be retried as a fresh embedded run.
- CLI docs keep the external-supervisor `timeout -k` hard-kill backstop guidance and call out the same SIGTERM-plus-grace-window shape for systemd units.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway-backed `openclaw agent` now treats `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT` as a caller abort, rejects the waiting request, and sends `chat.abort` for the accepted Gateway run id before the CLI exits. The same behavior is covered for default CLI identity and for `--model` override runs that require backend/admin abort authority.
- Real environment tested: remote Crabbox/GCP Spot VM, `e2-standard-8`, `us-east1-b`, lease `cbx_5e7f0ad24205` / slug `tidal-hermit`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`, PR head `533728f0a26596b5d315c527621075735456dbbf`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: on the remote VM, installed dependencies, ran `pnpm build`, started the built `dist/index.js` Gateway plus a temporary OpenAI-compatible provider that holds `/v1/responses` open, then ran the actual CLI twice: default Gateway CLI identity and `--model mock-openai/gpt-5.5`. After each provider request was in flight, the harness sent `SIGTERM` and verified the provider stream closed and the CLI exited with the Gateway abort error. The same VM then ran the focused Vitest files and `pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
proof.ok=true
proof.repo=/work/crabbox/cbx_5e7f0ad24205/openclaw-71710-sigterm
gateway.url=ws://127.0.0.1:42817
provider.url=http://127.0.0.1:35139/v1
provider.requests=2
default-cli.providerPath=/v1/responses
default-cli.providerClosed=true
default-cli.child.exit.code=1
default-cli.child.exit.signal=
default-cli.child.stderr.firstLine=AbortError: gateway request aborted for agent
model-override.providerPath=/v1/responses
model-override.providerClosed=true
model-override.child.exit.code=1
model-override.child.exit.signal=
model-override.child.stderr.firstLine=AbortError: gateway request aborted for agent
```

- Observed result after fix: both actual CLI runs received `SIGTERM`, aborted the waiting Gateway request, closed the in-flight provider response, and exited with `AbortError: gateway request aborted for agent`. The focused tests also verify the accepted run id/session key are used for `chat.abort`, that the model override abort path preserves backend/admin authority, that `--local` runs receive the same abort signal, and that abort errors do not enter embedded fallback classification.
- What was not tested: a live production provider credential, real cron/systemd timing, or an OS-level forced `SIGKILL`. The proof used a temporary OpenAI-compatible provider on the remote VM to avoid external credentials while exercising the built Gateway, actual CLI process, Gateway request identity, accepted-run callback, and abort path. The docs continue to recommend `timeout -k` or an equivalent supervisor backstop for hard-kill cases.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts`, `src/gateway/call.test.ts`, `src/gateway/client.watchdog.test.ts`.
- Scenario the tests lock in: accepted Gateway agent run plus `SIGTERM` interrupts the CLI, aborts the waiting request, sends `chat.abort` for the accepted run id, preserves the original Gateway identity for abort authorization, forwards the abort signal to local embedded runs, and removes signal listeners.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the three ownership boundaries changed by this PR without requiring a real production provider or long-running scheduler job.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw agent` now handles `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT` for Gateway-backed runs by interrupting the waiting request and asking the Gateway to abort an already accepted run id before exiting. External cron/systemd wrappers should still keep a hard-kill backstop such as `timeout -k 60 600 openclaw agent ...` or an equivalent SIGTERM grace window.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
external SIGTERM -> CLI listener absent -> Gateway request/run can remain in flight

After:
external SIGTERM -> CLI AbortSignal -> Gateway request rejects -> CLI chat.abort(runId) -> exit
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: on signal interruption only, the CLI may send an existing authenticated Gateway `chat.abort` request for the accepted run id and resolved session key. This reuses existing Gateway auth and abort semantics. Default runs use CLI identity; `--model` override runs use the same backend/admin authority as the original model override request so the abort is authorized without adding a new scope.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: remote Crabbox/GCP Linux VM for build, real Gateway/CLI/provider proof, focused tests, and `pnpm check:changed`.
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0` remotely.
- Model/provider: temporary OpenAI-compatible provider on the VM for real CLI/Gateway proof.
- Integration/channel: Gateway-backed `openclaw agent` CLI path.
- Relevant config: `OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS` unset for deterministic timeout expectations; Vitest workers limited to 1 for focused tests.

### Commands and results

```text
pnpm build
# passed

timeout 300s node .crabbox-proof/openclaw-84381-real-gateway-proof.mjs
# passed; proof.ok=true

env -u OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts
# 19 passed

env -u OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=60000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts
# 84 passed

env -u OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=60000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-client.config.ts src/gateway/client.watchdog.test.ts
# 11 passed

env -u OPENCLAW_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm check:changed
# exit 0; command=7m6.041s; total=7m37.28s
```

`pnpm check:changed` deliberately fail-safed to all lanes because the remote sync included the temporary untracked proof harness, so the remote gate was broader than the tracked PR diff.

### Expected

- CLI signal bridge aborts the waiting Gateway request and sends `chat.abort` for the accepted run id.
- Abort cleanup uses the same Gateway identity as the original accepted run.
- Local embedded runs receive the same abort signal instead of swallowing process signals.
- `callGateway` forwards `AbortSignal` and accepted callbacks into `GatewayClient.request`.
- Changed-file checks pass.

### Actual

- Real Gateway/CLI/provider proof passed for both default CLI identity and `--model` override identity.
- Focused tests passed.
- Remote `pnpm build` and `pnpm check:changed` passed.
- Crabbox release cleanup was checked afterward; no matching `crabbox-*` instances remained listed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified the issue is still current on `main`: server-side accepted-run abort machinery exists, but the one-shot `agent` CLI did not propagate process signals into the Gateway request or abort the accepted run.
- Verified the first implementation's cleanup authorization gap and fixed it by reusing the original Gateway request identity for `chat.abort`.
- Verified Claude's local/embedded abort concern against source and added a focused `--local` signal propagation test.
- Verified focused tests and real behavior proof on the exact pushed PR head `533728f0a26596b5d315c527621075735456dbbf`.

target.title: fix(codex): keep interrupted turns visible-answer eligible
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex app-server `turn.status: "interrupted"` was projected as an OpenClaw abort even when OpenClaw did not explicitly cancel the run.
- Solution: Keep app-server interrupted as terminal app-server state, but do not map it to OpenClaw `aborted` unless OpenClaw explicitly called `markAborted()` or `markTimedOut()`.
- What changed: Added projector and runner regressions for interrupted/tool-only/no-visible-answer Codex turns, including sparse successful bash output.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This did not treat every interrupted turn as success, did not synthesize final assistant text, did not disable cancellation, and did not change failed-turn error handling.

## Motivation

- Codex-backed dashboard turns can appear to stop after tool output without delivering the final visible assistant answer.
- The existing incomplete-turn/no-visible-answer guard was bypassed because the app-server `interrupted` terminal status was mapped to `aborted: true`.
- This materially affects UX for Codex-backed OpenClaw usage: the user sees tool-only progress and then needs a continuation turn instead of receiving the answer that the original user-facing turn owed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84492
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server interrupted turn status no longer suppresses no-visible-answer handling for user-facing turns.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev checkout rebased on `origin/main` plus Crabbox static SSH Linux VM. Private host/user/workroot values were redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
crabbox run --provider ssh --target linux --static-host <redacted-lab-host> \
  --static-user <redacted-user> --static-port 22 \
  --static-work-root <redacted-workroot> --shell -- \
  'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"; node --version; pnpm --version;
   pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts;
   CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts \
     extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts \
     -t "app-server interrupted status|sparse successful bash output";
   CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts \
     src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts \
     -t "sparse bash output"'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```shell
event-projector.test.ts: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 2 passed | 58 skipped
run.incomplete-turn.test.ts: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 1 passed | 95 skipped
run summary sync=2.536s command=32.891s total=35.452s exit=0
lease cleanup stopped=true policy=auto
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused interrupted-turn and sparse-bash regressions passed remotely through Crabbox.
- What was not tested: A full dashboard browser interaction was not run. Full monorepo tests were not run.
- Before evidence:

```shell
FAIL extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts > CodexAppServerEventProjector > does not treat app-server interrupted status as a user cancellation by itself
AssertionError: expected true to be false
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `CodexAppServerEventProjector` converted `turn.status === "interrupted"` into `this.aborted = true` and returned `aborted: this.aborted || turnInterrupted`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression covering an app-server interrupted terminal turn with no explicit OpenClaw cancellation and no visible assistant answer.
- Contributing context (if known): The embedded runner intentionally skips incomplete-turn/no-visible-answer handling for true aborts. That made the projector's over-broad status mapping user-visible.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - App-server `interrupted` does not by itself become OpenClaw `aborted`.
  - Sparse successful bash output plus no visible final assistant text remains eligible for no-visible-answer handling.
  - Replay-unsafe shell activity surfaces a verification warning instead of silently retrying or faking an answer.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug was a local projection/lifecycle mapping problem plus runner incomplete-turn classification; the focused tests cover both without live model nondeterminism.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

User-facing Codex turns that end with app-server `interrupted` but no explicit OpenClaw cancellation can now reach the existing no-visible-answer guard instead of being classified as aborted.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Codex turn/completed(status=interrupted) -> projector aborted=true -> incomplete-turn guard skipped -> no final visible answer handling

After:
Codex turn/completed(status=interrupted) -> projector aborted unchanged -> incomplete-turn guard remains active -> retry/error handling stays visible
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x64 local dev and Linux x64 Crabbox SSH VM
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.15.0` locally and on the VM
- Model/provider: Codex app-server/OpenAI Codex path modeled by focused projector tests
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw Codex app-server harness
- Relevant config (redacted): Crabbox static SSH provider with redacted host/user/workroot

### Steps

1. Rebased the branch on latest official `origin/main`.
2. Ran the focused local test files.
3. Ran the focused regression selections remotely through Crabbox static SSH.

### Expected

- App-server `interrupted` did not automatically become OpenClaw `aborted`.
- Sparse successful bash output did not count as a final visible assistant answer.
- Replay-unsafe tool activity surfaced the existing verification warning path.

### Actual

- Matched expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local focused tests after rebase:

```shell
CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  60 passed (60)
```

```shell
CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  96 passed (96)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Pre-fix focused regression failed on current `main`.
  - Local focused files passed after the patch and after rebase.
  - Crabbox SSH remote proof passed after rebase.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Explicit `markAborted()`/`markTimedOut()` paths remained separate.
  - Failed turns still set `promptError`.
  - Replay-unsafe sparse bash output surfaced a verification warning instead of being silently retried.
- What was not verified:
  - Full dashboard browser UX.
  - Full monorepo test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Some app-server `interrupted` statuses may correspond to genuine app-server-side interruptions.
  - Mitigation: The patch does not mark them successful and does not fake a reply; it only avoids treating them as OpenClaw/user aborts. Explicit OpenClaw abort and timeout paths still set `aborted`/`promptError`.

<!-- exact-interrupted-tool-only-proof-2026-05-20 -->

### Exact interrupted tool-only recovery proof

Added diagnostic regression coverage at commit `2010177bc7` for the exact path ClawSweeper requested:

- Codex app-server terminal projection: `turn/completed status=interrupted`.
- Prior turn item: successful bash `commandExecution` with empty aggregated output and `exitCode=0`.
- No assistant text was projected: `assistantTexts=[]`.
- OpenClaw projection after the patch: `aborted=false`, `timedOut=false`, bash tool metadata preserved.
- The no-visible-answer guard was exercised with a projector-compatible interrupted tool-only attempt and produced recovery text containing `couldn't generate a response`.
- Explicit cancellation counter-proof remained distinct: an explicitly aborted interrupted/tool-only attempt returned `null` from `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText(...)`, so user cancellation does not synthesize a fake final answer.

### Exact runtime interrupted tool-only recovery proof

After the focused tests, I also ran a runtime diagnostic through the real OpenClaw modules from commit `2010177bc7` rather than a Vitest assertion-only path. The script instantiated `CodexAppServerEventProjector`, fed a redacted Codex app-server `turn/completed` notification with `status="interrupted"`, one successful `commandExecution` item, `aggregatedOutput=""`, and `exitCode=0`, then passed the projected attempt into `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText(...)`.

target.title: fix(daemon): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff via systemd-run --scope
target.body:
## Summary

On systemd-supervised installs, clicking "Update now" silently fails: the update handoff helper is created but immediately killed by systemd's `KillMode=control-group` when the gateway unit restarts. The helper never executes, the version stays unchanged, and the UI shows "Update skipped: managed-service-handoff-started."

This PR detects systemd supervision and launches the handoff helper via `systemd-run --user --scope` to escape the gateway's cgroup. Non-systemd supervisors (launchd, etc.) continue using the existing detached-child path.

## Root cause

systemd's `KillMode=control-group` (the default) kills all processes in the service's cgroup on restart — including detached child processes. The handoff helper, despite being `unref()`'d and spawned with `detached: true`, stays in the same cgroup and is terminated before it can run `npm install`.

## Fix

```
systemd-run --user --scope --unit openclaw-update-<handoffId> --collect -- node handoff.cjs handoff.json
```

Key design choice: **`--scope` not `--service`**. This matters because:
- `--scope`: child **inherits the caller's environment** (profile, state dir, config, PATH, proxy, package manager discovery all preserved)
- `--service`: child runs in a **clean systemd environment** (would lose all service env vars)

The `env` object is built from `stripSupervisorHintEnv(process.env)` plus update-specific vars, and passed directly to `spawn()` — identical to the existing non-systemd path. Since `--scope` preserves caller env, no `--setenv` forwarding is needed.

Additional hardening:
- `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` strips non-`[a-zA-Z0-9:._-]` chars from `handoffId` to prevent `systemd-run` rejecting the unit name
- `/tmp/openclaw-update-in-progress` marker file for `ExecStartPre` defense-in-depth guard
- `--collect` flag ensures scope units are garbage-collected after exit

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `update-managed-service-handoff.ts` | Detect `supervisor === "systemd"` → spawn via `systemd-run --user --scope`; add marker file lifecycle; add `sanitizeSystemdUnitName()` |
| `update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` | 2 new tests: systemd path uses `systemd-run --scope`, non-systemd path uses plain detached node; env forwarding verified (no `--setenv` in argv, env via spawn options) |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts
 ✓ gateway-client (7 tests) 640ms
 ✓ gateway-methods (7 tests) 634ms
 ✓ daemon (4 tests) 310ms
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: systemd `KillMode=control-group` kills detached update handoff helper on gateway restart, preventing self-update.
- **Real environment tested**: OrbStack Linux VM (Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy, kernel 6.19.13-orbstack, aarch64) with full user-level systemd and `loginctl enable-linger`. Node.js v24.15.0.

### Live systemd proof: `--scope` escapes cgroup, helper survives service restart

Created a systemd user service with `KillMode=control-group` (matching OpenClaw's generated unit at `src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts:83`):

```ini
[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/tmp/handoff-test/gateway-sim.sh
KillMode=control-group
Restart=no
```

**Test A: WITH `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR)**

```
=== Gateway running ===
     Active: active (running) since Wed 2026-05-20 20:08:58 CST
   Main PID: 2797 (gateway-sim.sh)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/openclaw-gateway-test.service

=== Helper started (in separate scope unit, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2799 started in scope at Wed May 20 08:08:58 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s while gateway lives...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:00 ===
Gateway service stopped

=== Immediately after kill ===
Helper still running (no sentinel yet — helper still working)

=== After 10s wait (helper finished at 20:09:06) ===
Helper: still alive after 8s! Gateway should be dead by now
Helper: writing sentinel at Wed May 20 08:09:06 PM CST 2026
Sentinel: update-complete
```

**Result**: Helper survived gateway service kill and completed work ✅

**Test B: WITHOUT `systemd-run --scope` (current main behavior)**

Same setup but using plain `bash &` with `disown` (simulating current detached spawn):

```
=== Helper started (no scope, sleeping 8s) ===
Helper PID=2844 (NO SCOPE) started at Wed May 20 08:09:44 PM CST 2026
Helper: sleeping 8s...

=== KILLING GATEWAY (KillMode=control-group) at 20:09:46 ===

=== After 10s wait ===
Helper log: stopped at "sleeping 8s..." — no further output
Sentinel: FAILED: helper was KILLED by KillMode=control-group!
```

**Result**: Helper was killed when gateway service stopped ❌

### Comparison

| Scenario | Helper survives service stop? | Sentinel written? |
|----------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| `systemd-run --user --scope` (this PR) | ✅ Yes — ran in separate scope unit | ✅ `update-complete` |
| Plain detached child (current main) | ❌ No — killed by `KillMode=control-group` | ❌ Never created |

### Security: env via spawn(), not argv

```typescript
// Line 352: env passed to spawn() for systemd-run
child = spawn("systemd-run", [...], { cwd: params.root, env, detached: true, stdio: "ignore" });
//                                                         ^^^ full env via spawn option
```

`systemd-run --scope` inherits caller env (unlike `--service` which runs in a clean env). No `--setenv=KEY=VALUE` is used — secrets stay in process environment (`/proc/PID/environ`, restricted to same UID), not in command-line arguments (`/proc/PID/cmdline`, world-readable).

Test coverage verifies this explicitly:
```typescript
// env is passed via spawn options, not --setenv argv
expect(options.env).toBeDefined();
expect(args).not.toContain("--setenv");
```

### What was not tested

- Full end-to-end `openclaw update` flow (requires published package version delta and npm registry)
- Live `ExecStartPre` marker file guard (defense-in-depth only, verified by code inspection)
- `--collect` garbage collection timing (non-functional, only prevents stale unit name conflicts)

Closes #84068

target.title: fix(codex): keep interrupted turns visible-answer eligible
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex app-server `turn.status: "interrupted"` was projected as an OpenClaw abort even when OpenClaw did not explicitly cancel the run.
- Solution: Keep app-server interrupted as terminal app-server state, but do not map it to OpenClaw `aborted` unless OpenClaw explicitly called `markAborted()` or `markTimedOut()`.
- What changed: Added projector and runner regressions for interrupted/tool-only/no-visible-answer Codex turns, including sparse successful bash output.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This did not treat every interrupted turn as success, did not synthesize final assistant text, did not disable cancellation, and did not change failed-turn error handling.

## Motivation

- Codex-backed dashboard turns can appear to stop after tool output without delivering the final visible assistant answer.
- The existing incomplete-turn/no-visible-answer guard was bypassed because the app-server `interrupted` terminal status was mapped to `aborted: true`.
- This materially affects UX for Codex-backed OpenClaw usage: the user sees tool-only progress and then needs a continuation turn instead of receiving the answer that the original user-facing turn owed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84492
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server interrupted turn status no longer suppresses no-visible-answer handling for user-facing turns.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev checkout rebased on `origin/main` plus Crabbox static SSH Linux VM. Private host/user/workroot values were redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
crabbox run --provider ssh --target linux --static-host <redacted-lab-host> \
  --static-user <redacted-user> --static-port 22 \
  --static-work-root <redacted-workroot> --shell -- \
  'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"; node --version; pnpm --version;
   pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts;
   CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts \
     extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts \
     -t "app-server interrupted status|sparse successful bash output";
   CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts \
     src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts \
     -t "sparse bash output"'
```

- Evidence after fix:

```shell
event-projector.test.ts: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 2 passed | 58 skipped
run.incomplete-turn.test.ts: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 1 passed | 95 skipped
run summary sync=2.536s command=32.891s total=35.452s exit=0
lease cleanup stopped=true policy=auto
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused interrupted-turn and sparse-bash regressions passed remotely through Crabbox.
- What was not tested: A full dashboard browser interaction was not run. Full monorepo tests were not run.
- Before evidence:

```shell
FAIL extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts > CodexAppServerEventProjector > does not treat app-server interrupted status as a user cancellation by itself
AssertionError: expected true to be false
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `CodexAppServerEventProjector` converted `turn.status === "interrupted"` into `this.aborted = true` and returned `aborted: this.aborted || turnInterrupted`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression covering an app-server interrupted terminal turn with no explicit OpenClaw cancellation and no visible assistant answer.
- Contributing context (if known): The embedded runner intentionally skips incomplete-turn/no-visible-answer handling for true aborts. That made the projector's over-broad status mapping user-visible.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - App-server `interrupted` does not by itself become OpenClaw `aborted`.
  - Sparse successful bash output plus no visible final assistant text remains eligible for no-visible-answer handling.
  - Replay-unsafe shell activity surfaces a verification warning instead of silently retrying or faking an answer.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug was a local projection/lifecycle mapping problem plus runner incomplete-turn classification; the focused tests cover both without live model nondeterminism.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

User-facing Codex turns that end with app-server `interrupted` but no explicit OpenClaw cancellation can now reach the existing no-visible-answer guard instead of being classified as aborted.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Codex turn/completed(status=interrupted) -> projector aborted=true -> incomplete-turn guard skipped -> no final visible answer handling

After:
Codex turn/completed(status=interrupted) -> projector aborted unchanged -> incomplete-turn guard remains active -> retry/error handling stays visible
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x64 local dev and Linux x64 Crabbox SSH VM
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.15.0` locally and on the VM
- Model/provider: Codex app-server/OpenAI Codex path modeled by focused projector tests
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw Codex app-server harness
- Relevant config (redacted): Crabbox static SSH provider with redacted host/user/workroot

### Steps

1. Rebased the branch on latest official `origin/main`.
2. Ran the focused local test files.
3. Ran the focused regression selections remotely through Crabbox static SSH.

### Expected

- App-server `interrupted` did not automatically become OpenClaw `aborted`.
- Sparse successful bash output did not count as a final visible assistant answer.
- Replay-unsafe tool activity surfaced the existing verification warning path.

### Actual

- Matched expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local focused tests after rebase:

```shell
CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  60 passed (60)
```

```shell
CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  96 passed (96)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Pre-fix focused regression failed on current `main`.
  - Local focused files passed after the patch and after rebase.
  - Crabbox SSH remote proof passed after rebase.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Explicit `markAborted()`/`markTimedOut()` paths remained separate.
  - Failed turns still set `promptError`.
  - Replay-unsafe sparse bash output surfaced a verification warning instead of being silently retried.
- What was not verified:
  - Full dashboard browser UX.
  - Full monorepo test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Some app-server `interrupted` statuses may correspond to genuine app-server-side interruptions.
  - Mitigation: The patch does not mark them successful and does not fake a reply; it only avoids treating them as OpenClaw/user aborts. Explicit OpenClaw abort and timeout paths still set `aborted`/`promptError`.

<!-- exact-interrupted-tool-only-proof-2026-05-20 -->

### Exact interrupted tool-only recovery proof

Added diagnostic regression coverage at commit `2010177bc7` for the exact path ClawSweeper requested:

- Codex app-server terminal projection: `turn/completed status=interrupted`.
- Prior turn item: successful bash `commandExecution` with empty aggregated output and `exitCode=0`.
- No assistant text was projected: `assistantTexts=[]`.
- OpenClaw projection after the patch: `aborted=false`, `timedOut=false`, bash tool metadata preserved.
- The no-visible-answer guard was exercised with a projector-compatible interrupted tool-only attempt and produced recovery text containing `couldn't generate a response`.
- Explicit cancellation counter-proof remained distinct: an explicitly aborted interrupted/tool-only attempt returned `null` from `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText(...)`, so user cancellation does not synthesize a fake final answer.

Focused proof commands run locally:

```bash
CI=1 timeout 240s /home/probo/.local/share/pnpm/store/v11/links/@/node/24.15.0/73ce00437ab4f9c458daa7174613927ead616d9a77c35c8b15545d4dc2ac94a0/node_modules/node/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
# Test Files 1 passed (1)
# Tests 61 passed (61)

CI=1 timeout 240s /home/probo/.local/share/pnpm/store/v11/links/@/node/24.15.0/73ce00437ab4f9c458daa7174613927ead616d9a77c35c8b15545d4dc2ac94a0/node_modules/node/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -t "app-server interrupted tool-only output"
# Test Files 1 passed (1)
# Tests 1 passed | 96 skipped (97)

CI=1 timeout 240s /home/probo/.local/share/pnpm/store/v11/links/@/node/24.15.0/73ce00437ab4f9c458daa7174613927ead616d9a77c35c8b15545d4dc2ac94a0/node_modules/node/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "keeps upstream cancellation aborted"
# Test Files 1 passed (1)
# Tests 1 passed | 185 skipped (186)
```

Crabbox SSH proof on the lab VM also passed for the exact diagnostic pair:

```bash
CRABBOX_CONFIG=/home/probo/.config/crabbox/lab-ssh.yaml CRABBOX_SSH_KEY=/home/probo/.ssh/crabbox_lab_ed25519 timeout 1200s /home/probo/.local/bin/crabbox run --provider ssh --target linux --static-host [redacted] --static-user [redacted] --static-port 22 --static-work-root /home/[redacted]/crabbox --shell -- 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"; CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts -t "sparse successful bash output|explicit cancellation marked aborted"; CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -t "app-server interrupted tool-only output"'
# event-projector: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 2 passed | 59 skipped (61)
# incomplete-turn: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 96 skipped (97)
# lease cleanup stopped=true
```


<!-- exact-runtime-interrupted-tool-only-proof-2026-05-20 -->

### Exact runtime interrupted tool-only recovery proof

After the focused tests, I also ran a runtime diagnostic through the real OpenClaw modules from commit `2010177bc7` rather than a Vitest assertion-only path. The script instantiated `CodexAppServerEventProjector`, fed a redacted Codex app-server `turn/completed` notification with `status="interrupted"`, one successful `commandExecution` item, `aggregatedOutput=""`, and `exitCode=0`, then passed the projected attempt into `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText(...)`.

Redacted local runtime output:

```text
[runtime-proof] event=turn/completed status=interrupted item=commandExecution command=bash aggregatedOutputLength=0 exitCode=0
[runtime-proof] projected aborted=false timedOut=false assistantTextCount=0 toolMetas=[{"toolName":"bash","meta":"ps -eo pid,ppid,stat,cmd | rg 'venv-roadmap|pytest|run_security_contract_validation|validate_public_install|git push|ap… (workspace)"}]
[runtime-proof] noVisibleAnswerRecovery="⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response. Please try again."
[runtime-proof] explicitCancellation projectedAborted=true recovery=null
```

Redacted Crabbox static SSH runtime output from the lab VM:

```text
[runtime-proof] event=turn/completed status=interrupted item=commandExecution command=bash aggregatedOutputLength=0 exitCode=0
[runtime-proof] projected aborted=false timedOut=false assistantTextCount=0 toolMetas=[{"toolName":"bash","meta":"ps -eo pid,ppid,stat,cmd | rg 'venv-roadmap|pytest|run_security_contract_validation|validate_public_install|git push|ap… (workspace)"}]
[runtime-proof] noVisibleAnswerRecovery="⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response. Please try again."
[runtime-proof] explicitCancellation projectedAborted=true recovery=null
command complete in 25.156s total=27.671s
run summary sync=2.496s command=25.156s total=27.671s sync_skipped=false exit=0
lease cleanup stopped=true policy=auto
```

This is the exact after-fix runtime path requested by ClawSweeper: app-server `interrupted`, tool-only/no-assistant, no explicit OpenClaw abort, no timeout, no synthesized assistant answer, and the existing no-visible-answer recovery text remained reachable. The explicit-cancellation counter-proof stayed distinct: after `markAborted()`, the same interrupted/tool-only shape projected `aborted=true` and produced `recovery=null`.

target.title: [codex] fix: omit provider api keys from generated models.json
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#11829.

This prevents generated `models.json` files from persisting provider-level `apiKey` fields. The sanitization happens at the serialization boundary, after provider discovery/normalization/merge, so provider catalog metadata remains available while credentials stay out of the prompt-visible model catalog.

Covered cases:
- implicit provider env markers, such as `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- literal configured provider keys
- stale provider keys preserved from an existing `models.json` in merge mode

## Root cause

`planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps()` built the final provider catalog and serialized it directly. Earlier steps could add `apiKey` values or preserve them from an existing `models.json`, so secrets or secret markers were written back into the agent model catalog.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs $(rg --files src/agents | rg 'models-config.*\.test\.ts$')`

target.title: fix(telegram): avoid silent truncation in partial streaming finals
target.body:
Fixes an intermittent Telegram partial-streaming failure mode where a streamed final could be marked as delivered before any follow-up chunks are actually sent.

When a follow-up send fails, the channel code can otherwise suppress the visible error fallback (because deliveryState already says delivered), leaving the user with a mid-sentence cutoff.

Changes:
- Telegram: only mark streamed finals delivered after follow-up chunks are sent successfully; add regression tests.
- Telegram: when a preview-finalized final looks truncated, try to repair it by reading the transcript-backed canonical final and editing the Telegram message to the longer text.
- Diagnostics: avoid aborting queued embedded work when the session is still processing queued items (reduces false-positive stuck aborts).

Related: #49889 (Telegram partial-stream finalization delivery-state ambiguity / message-loss incidents).

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): bound compile page reads
target.body:
## Summary
- Bound Memory Wiki page-summary reads during compile with the existing concurrency helper.
- Added a regression test that asserts compile never opens more than 16 page reads at once.
- Added an Unreleased changelog entry.

Fixes #68738.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Memory Wiki `wiki compile` now bounds compile-time source page-summary reads so a large FileProvider-backed vault does not launch an unbounded `fs.readFile` burst while compiling.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS under `/Users/.../Documents/Codex/openclaw-openclaw-https-github-com-openclaw-2`, using an isolated proof vault at `/Users/.../Documents/Codex/openclaw-memory-wiki-proof-20260520-1950/vault`, `memory-wiki` config, native render mode, Obsidian disabled, and 180 real Markdown source pages (`sources/proof-001.md` through `sources/proof-180.md`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the source-checkout CLI entrypoint equivalent to `openclaw wiki compile` after applying this patch:

```text
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$PROOF_ROOT/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$PROOF_ROOT/state \
  NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 \
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts wiki compile
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the real source checkout run:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$PROOF_ROOT/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$PROOF_ROOT/state \
  NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 \
  node --import tsx src/entry.ts wiki compile
Compiled wiki vault at $PROOF_ROOT/vault (189 pages, 0 indexes updated).
real 31.94
user 29.46
sys 1.90
```

- **Observed result after fix**: First JSON compile completed successfully and produced the expected compiled vault counts, then the rerun completed without errors:

```text
pageCounts={"source":180,"report":9,"entity":0,"concept":0,"synthesis":0}
totalPages=189
exit=0
```

- **What was not tested**: I did not run a live Obsidian app integration or a private production vault. The after-fix proof used a local Documents/Codex OpenClaw setup with a synthetic 180-source-page vault, plus the focused automated checks below.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json --type-aware --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 corepack pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.ts extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

target.title: WORKING: All Microsoft Issues and PRs (refresh)
target.body:
# Microsoft Ecosystem Issues & PRs Tracker

> **Purpose:** Living checklist for maintainers to track all open Microsoft-related issues and PRs (Teams, Windows, WSL, Azure, M365/SharePoint).
>
> **How to use:**
>
> - Mark items resolved by editing this PR body and changing `[ ]` to `[x]`
> - Claim items by adding your GitHub handle to the `Assignee` column
> - Priority guide: **P0** = crash/blocker/security, **P1** = significant bug/regression, **P2** = minor bug/enhancement, **P3** = nice-to-have/stale
> - Items marked _(stale)_ have been flagged by the stale bot due to inactivity
>
> **Last updated:** 2026-05-20 (restored closed counts after post-purge audit refresh)

---

| Category | Issues | PRs | Total | Closed | Remaining |

| Azure | 8 | 6 | 14 | 2 | 12 |
| SharePoint / M365 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 |

commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: perf: isolate doctor core check tests
target.body:
## Summary
- isolate doctor core health-check unit tests from real skills/workspace/plugin discovery
- add an internal health-check dependency seam plus a lazy runtime boundary for real skills readiness detection
- keep one real e2e smoke proving the missing workspace skill failure and repair path

## Root Cause
`src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` looked like a unit test, but several cases executed real doctor checks. That pulled in skills runtime, workspace discovery, plugin skills, and filesystem paths, making the file dominate unit test perf.

## Impact
The unit test now covers registry, conversion, scoping, and repair behavior with lightweight fakes. The real filesystem/skills proof remains covered in `doctor-core-checks.e2e.test.ts` outside the unit perf group.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts -- --reporter=default` -> 6 passed, test time 18ms
- `pnpm test src/flows/doctor-core-checks.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=default` -> 1 passed, real skills smoke 523ms
- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts -- --reporter=default` -> 9 passed
- `pnpm test:perf:imports src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts -- --reporter=default` -> no skills/workspace/plugin-skills chain in top imports
- `pnpm test:perf:groups` -> `src/flows` 4.8ms; `doctor-core-checks.test.ts` no longer top 25
- `pnpm check:changed` -> passed
- `pnpm format:check src/commands/doctor-skills.ts src/commands/doctor-skills-core.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.e2e.test.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm build` -> passed

No changelog entry: internal test/perf refactor only.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds auto-reply fallback cleanup and regression tests to clear auto-owned session model pins after restart, abort, drain, lane-cleared, timeout, or client-closed fallback paths.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main persists auto fallback selections and has no cleanup on the ephemeral success/abort paths this PR targets, while the PR body supplies redacted live terminal proof of stuck Telegram session pins and after-fix cleanup.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can be attempted because the blocker is a bounded matcher and regression-test gap in the two touched files.

target.title: fix: clear auto fallback pins after ephemeral primary failures
target.body:
## Summary

Clears auto-owned model fallback pins when the primary model failure was ephemeral, so a session is not silently pinned to a fallback provider after restart/drain/client-closed/timeout paths.

## Problem

If the primary run is interrupted by gateway restart/drain/lane clearing/user abort, or if Codex/OpenAI primary fails with a transient client-closed/timeout style error and fallback succeeds, OpenClaw can persist an auto fallback model override on the session. That makes future turns continue on the fallback even after the primary provider is healthy again.

## Fix

- Detect auto-owned fallback selections that originated from the current primary run.
- Clear that auto fallback state on restart/drain/lane-cleared/user-abort exits.
- Clear it after successful fallback when all primary attempts look ephemeral.
- Preserve explicit user-selected model pins.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Auto-owned fallback model pins persisted after restart/client-closed/timeout instability, leaving live Telegram topic sessions on a fallback provider even after the selected/default model remained `openai/gpt-5.5`.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Gateway on macOS with Telegram topic sessions, Codex runtime, `openai/gpt-5.5` selected, and subscription-backed `openai-codex` provider active. Topic identifiers and session keys are redacted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit all --json \
  | jq '[active Telegram group/topic sessions pinned to deepseek/minimax] | length'

openclaw agent \
  --agent openclaw \
  --session-id smoke-gpt55-codex-pr-proof-redacted \
  --model openai/gpt-5.5 \
  --message 'Reply exactly SMOKE_OK and nothing else.' \
  --json --timeout 60
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the real local OpenClaw setup after applying this patch as temporary local containment and restarting safely:

```text
$ openclaw sessions --all-agents --limit all --json | jq '[active Telegram group/topic sessions pinned to deepseek/minimax] | length'
0

$ openclaw agent --agent openclaw --session-id smoke-gpt55-codex-pr-proof-redacted --model openai/gpt-5.5 --message 'Reply exactly SMOKE_OK and nothing else.' --json --timeout 60
{
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "result": {
    "payloads": [
      { "text": "SMOKE_OK", "mediaUrl": null }
    ],
    "meta": {
      "agentMeta": {
        "provider": "openai-codex",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "agentHarnessId": "codex"
      },
      "aborted": false
    }
  }
}
```

Before cleanup, the same live environment had two active Telegram topic sessions auto-pinned to fallback after restart/client-closed instability:

```text
active Telegram topic sessions pinned to fallback: 2
selected/default model: openai/gpt-5.5
active fallback model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash
likely failure path: forced restart / reply operation aborted for restart, followed by Codex app-server client closed / LLM request timed out
```

**Observed result after fix:** Active Telegram fallback pins dropped to `0`, and a fresh GPT-5.5 Codex smoke returned `SMOKE_OK` through provider `openai-codex`, model `gpt-5.5`, harness `codex`, with `aborted: false`. This verifies the live session state was no longer stuck on DeepSeek/MiniMax fallback and the primary Codex-backed GPT-5.5 route was healthy after the cleanup.

**What was not tested:** A synthetic end-to-end restart-triggered fallback failure inside GitHub Actions was not tested because it requires a live OpenClaw gateway/session environment with Telegram topic state and Codex auth. Regression tests cover the clearing semantics and user-selected pin preservation.

## Related fallback-pin policy

This PR is intentionally narrow. It clears only auto-owned fallback pins tied to ephemeral failure paths for the current primary run. It does not replace broader TTL/config fallback-pin policy work; maintainers can still decide whether persistent auto fallback should later become configurable or time-limited. The aim here is to prevent transient restart/client-closed/timeout events from silently pinning a session to fallback forever.

target.title: Fix stale WebChat typing indicator after terminal session patch
target.body:
## Summary

- clear local WebChat run state when an applied `sessions.changed` patch marks the current session terminal
- keep the existing full `sessions.list` reconciliation path using the same helper
- add regression coverage for a terminal session patch clearing `chatRunId` and an empty `chatStream`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat could keep showing the typing indicator after the final assistant response was already rendered and the Gateway session was terminal.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from the PR 70543 review worktree, Control UI at `http://localhost:19011/`, Gateway at `ws://127.0.0.1:19010`, provider/model `atlassian-aigw/gpt-5.5-2026-04-23`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: reproduced the stuck UI with a WebChat exec-denial turn, queried `sessions.list` and `chat.history` through a local Gateway client, then added and ran the focused controller regression test in this branch.

Evidence after fix: the new regression test first failed with `expected 'run-1' to be null`; after the patch, `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` passed with 29 tests.

Observed result after fix: applying a terminal `sessions.changed` patch for the active session now clears `chatRunId`, `chatStream`, and `chatStreamStartedAt`, removing the stale typing indicator state.

What was not tested: a rebuilt browser bundle against the live local Gateway after this branch was split into its own worktree.

target.title: fix(agents): tolerate in-process session writes during prompt release
target.body:
## Summary

- Track exact same-process, lock-owned session-file fingerprints across embedded attempt controllers.
- Allow a released prompt attempt to refresh its fence only when the current file fingerprint exactly matches a later explicit OpenClaw-owned transcript write.
- Preserve fail-closed takeover detection for unowned external session-file changes.
- Forward the owned-write publication flag through the production `promptActiveSession` transcript context.
- Keep broad same-process callbacks and cleanup-lock release from publishing globally trusted fingerprints.

## Root Cause

The embedded attempt session fence only compared the session file's filesystem fingerprint saved at `releaseForPrompt()` against the fingerprint observed after the prompt lock was reacquired. That correctly rejects unowned external edits, but it also rejects legitimate OpenClaw-managed transcript appends from another same-process controller/attempt that acquired the session write lock while the first attempt was released for model I/O.

The fix keeps the fingerprint fence, but makes it ownership-aware: OpenClaw records a globally owned post-write fingerprint only for explicit owned transcript writes routed through `runWithOwnedSessionTranscriptWriteLock()`, and only when the pre-write fingerprint is already trusted by the process. A released attempt accepts the change only when the current file fingerprint exactly equals a newer owned fingerprint. Direct external edits, external edits followed by a broad locked append, and external edits interleaved while cleanup holds the lock still fail closed.

Fixes #84059.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A prompt-released embedded attempt no longer throws `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` when another controller publishes an explicit owned transcript write for the same session file. External unowned file changes still throw, including an external edit interleaved during a broad same-process locked callback and an external edit interleaved while another controller holds the cleanup lock.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at original proof head `7008438d978c75a21e811df4e2dc041d9f9dfe71`, then rebased/verified again through current PR head `6e73d291698bd794d813e8f7e611eceda7d4a29a`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.15.0`, real temp session files on the local filesystem, real OpenClaw `acquireSessionWriteLock`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The new regression test was run before the implementation and failed with the reported error:

```text
AssertionError: promise rejected "EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: sess..." instead of resolving

Caused by: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: .../session.jsonl
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
node --import tsx ../proof-embedded-session-lock-in-process.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
OpenClaw proof checkout: /Users/tianxiao/Documents/Codex/2026-05-19/openclaw-message-tool-bug/openclaw-community
In-process locked write result: post-write-committed
In-process takeover detected: false
In-process session line count: 3
External direct write error: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /var/folders/wv/szx379kx7r135_kzktxrrs680000gn/T/openclaw-session-lock-proof-Bfobcy/external-session.jsonl
External takeover detected: true
External session line count: 2
External plus locked write error: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /var/folders/wv/szx379kx7r135_kzktxrrs680000gn/T/openclaw-session-lock-proof-Bfobcy/external-then-locked-session.jsonl
External plus locked takeover detected: true
External plus locked session line count: 3
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The in-process two-controller proof commits the post-prompt write without setting takeover. Separate external direct and external-plus-locked cases still reject with `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, proving a locked append does not launder an earlier external edit. The latest unit regression also proves cleanup-lock release does not launder an external edit into a globally owned fingerprint.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run a live Feishu, Slack, WebChat, or Discord channel smoke. The proof uses the embedded session-lock controller directly with real filesystem writes to reproduce the source-level race without sending live channel messages.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` -> 19 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` -> 3 files, 60 passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Security-boundary update

- Added a production-shaped prompt context regression so the `publishOwnedWrite` option is forwarded to `sessionLockController.withSessionWriteLock()`.
- Removed cleanup-lock release fingerprint publication; cleanup no longer marks changed final session-file state as globally owned.
- Added a regression for an external append interleaved while another controller holds cleanup lock.
- Ordinary broad `withSessionWriteLock()` callbacks still refresh their own controller fence, but no longer publish a globally owned fingerprint for other released controllers to accept.

target.title: fix(webchat): hide legacy runtime transcript wrappers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: legacy session history can already contain runtime-only prompts as quoted internal-context wrappers, `System: [...] Background task ...` status lines, or gateway restart `System:` status blocks, which still show up in WebChat/history after the newer write-path fix.
- Why it matters: these rows are not user-authored transcript content and can expose internal task metadata/noise in visible chat surfaces.
- What changed: `chat.history` projection now unwraps legacy quote wrappers only when the inner text is a known runtime prompt, strips standalone or prefixed background-task status lines including end-of-string cases, strips gateway restart status blocks, hides now-empty visible history rows, and preserves following real user text.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not rewrite transcript files and does not change the newer persistence path fixed by #71229.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #71229
- Related #71312
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the history sanitizer only recognized internal context delimiters when the marker began at a real line boundary, so quote-wrapped legacy rows bypassed the existing internal-context stripper. Background-task and gateway-restart status rows also were not modeled as legacy runtime-only transcript text.
- Missing detection / guardrail: regression coverage did not include quote-wrapped persisted runtime prompts, standalone background-task status text, failed background-task prefixes, gateway restart status blocks, or truncated internal-context completion payloads.
- Contributing context (if known): #71229 fixed the forward write path, but legacy persisted rows still need a display projection when existing sessions are read.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: quote-wrapped failed ACP internal-context wrappers are omitted from history; standalone background-task status without a trailing newline is omitted; mixed background-task status plus a real user line preserves only the real user text; gateway restart status blocks preserve only the following real user text; truncated quote-wrapped internal-context completion rows, full subagent completion internal-context events, and quote-wrapped startup prompts are omitted.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `buildSessionHistorySnapshot` is the Gateway history projection path used before WebChat/history delivery.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing legacy internal-envelope test covered unquoted delimiter blocks only.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Existing WebChat/history views no longer show legacy runtime-only rows that were already persisted as quoted internal-context prompts, background-task status lines, or gateway restart status blocks.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[legacy transcript row] -> [chat.history] -> [internal status text visible]

After:
[legacy transcript row] -> [history projection] -> [hidden if runtime-only]
[background/gateway status + real text] -> [history projection] -> [real text only]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebChat/history
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Build a session history snapshot from a persisted user row containing a quote-wrapped `<<<BEGIN_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>...<<<END_OPENCLAW_INTERNAL_CONTEXT>>>` failed ACP/subagent event.
2. Build a snapshot from a persisted user row containing only `System: [timestamp] Background task done: ...` with no trailing newline.
3. Build a snapshot from a persisted user row containing a failed background-task status followed by `[Sat 2026-04-25 ...] 检查codex 状态`.
4. Build a snapshot from a persisted user row containing a gateway restart status block followed by `[Sat 2026-04-25 ...] 好了吗`.
5. Build a snapshot from the quote-wrapped ACP Codex gateway restart sample ending in `[Sat 2026-04-25 17:21 GMT+8] 跑真实 assistant output smoke，不只看 session created。`.
6. Build a snapshot from the quote-wrapped Codex review sample with a `Background task done` prefix followed by `[Sat 2026-04-25 17:23 GMT+8] 你和codex 沟通审视：https://github.com/openai/codex`.
7. Build a snapshot from the quote-wrapped, truncated ACP internal-context completion sample that includes the Codex review result body.
8. Build a snapshot from the quote-wrapped `Background task done` prefix followed by `[Sat 2026-04-25 17:44 GMT+8] continue`.

### Expected

- Runtime-only rows are omitted from visible history.
- Mixed rows preserve only the real user text, e.g. `升级前 5 分钟 checklist`, `检查codex 状态`, `好了吗`, `跑真实 assistant output smoke，不只看 session created。`, `你和codex 沟通审视：https://github.com/openai/codex`, or `continue`.
- Raw transcript sequence tracking still accounts for the raw rows.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Red test first: `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 pnpm test src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts` failed on the 3 original regressions before the fix.
  - New sample red test: gateway restart status block failed before the follow-up sanitizer patch; failed background-task prefix already passed with the background-task stripper.
  - Exact ACP Codex smoke sample: direct projection produced only `跑真实 assistant output smoke，不只看 session created。`; then this exact quote-wrapped payload was added as a regression test.
  - Exact Codex review samples: direct projection produced only `你和codex 沟通审视：https://github.com/openai/codex`; then the background-task prefix and truncated internal-context completion payload were added as regression tests.
  - Exact continue sample: direct projection produced only `continue`; then this exact quote-wrapped payload was added as a regression test.
  - Exact full subagent completion internal-context sample: projection produced no visible history rows; then this payload was added as a regression test.
  - Exact quote-wrapped startup prompt sample: projection produced no visible history rows; then this payload was added as a regression test.
  - Gateway targeted tests after latest regression: `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/chat-sanitize.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts` passed, 3 files / 80 tests.
  - Direct smoke via `pnpm exec tsx` produced `{ rawTranscriptSeq: 3, texts: ["升级前 5 分钟 checklist"] }` for the original legacy shapes.
  - After rebasing onto latest `origin/main`, the previously failing auto-reply CI shard files also passed locally: `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE=0 pnpm test src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/chat-sanitize.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`, 5 files / 72 tests.
  - Broad local `check:changed` was attempted after dependency refresh: conflict markers, core typechecks, core lint, import cycles, and auth/webhook guards passed; the broad changed-test lane hit local Vitest worker OOM after 211 files / 2703 tests passed. A high-heap retry avoided OOM but hit the test runner no-output timeout. CI is the source of truth for the full changed sweep.
- Edge cases checked: quote-wrapped internal context, truncated internal context, background status with no trailing newline, failed background status plus real user text, gateway restart block plus real user text, quote-wrapped ACP Codex gateway restart smoke request, Codex review request with URL, quote-wrapped continue request, full subagent completion internal-context event, quote-wrapped startup prompt, mixed background status plus real user text, heartbeat/internal-only history filtering from latest main, directive-only assistant send regression.
- What you did **not** verify: live installed Gateway/WebChat UI against a real user profile.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: over-stripping user-authored text that happens to look like runtime status.
  - Mitigation: quote unwrapping only applies when the inner text matches known legacy runtime prompts; background-task stripping is limited to the exact legacy `System: [timestamp] Background task ...:` prefix shape; gateway restart stripping is limited to the exact legacy `System: [timestamp] Gateway restart...` block shape and preserves following user text.
## CI Note

- This PR also includes a two-line macOS SwiftFormat cleanup in `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecAllowlistMatcher.swift` and `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecApprovals.swift` to clear the current `macos-swift` CI failure inherited from latest `origin/main`; no behavior change.

target.title: Dockerfile: add python -> python3 symlink for bookworm
target.body:
## Summary

Add a conditional `python` → `python3` symlink to every Debian-based OpenClaw Dockerfile so agents that default to `python` (rather than `python3`) don't waste a tool round trip on `command not found`.

## Problem

Debian bookworm dropped the unversioned `python` command. Every OpenClaw image that ships `python3` — whether via the base image, the `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` apt list, or a per-solution extension Dockerfile — inherits the missing symlink.

LLM agents pretrained on a corpus where `python` is the canonical invocation reach for `python` first. Inside an OpenClaw container, that's:

1. Tool call: `python some-script.py`
2. Response: `/bin/sh: python: command not found`
3. Reasoning + retry: `python3 some-script.py`

Roughly two extra tool turns and a few hundred wasted tokens of conversation churn per attempt. Across every Python-using session, for every OpenClaw user, on every bookworm-based image, this is a steady, measurable, free-to-fix token tax. The underlying debian-conformance issue is upstream's; what this PR addresses is the gap between agent behaviour priors and image reality.

## Fix

All four Dockerfiles (`Dockerfile`, `Dockerfile.sandbox`, `Dockerfile.sandbox-browser`, `Dockerfile.sandbox-common`) get the same conditional pattern:

```dockerfile
RUN if command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ ! -e /usr/local/bin/python ]; then \
      ln -sf /usr/bin/python3 /usr/local/bin/python; \
    fi
```

- No-op for slim variants that don't ship python3.
- No-op if an operator has installed `python-is-python3` (the Debian-blessed alternative) — their symlink wins.
- No-op on re-build (idempotent).

## Why not `python-is-python3`?

Reasonable question. The apt package is the dpkg-blessed answer and would work for most images. I went with the conditional symlink instead because:

1. **Slim variants** may not have python3 at all. `python-is-python3` would fail an apt install there; the conditional symlink gracefully no-ops.
2. **Gateway's optional apt layer** — the main `Dockerfile`'s apt-install step is already conditional on `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` being set. Adding `python-is-python3` as a default apt dependency would force an apt round-trip that isn't there today.
3. **Base-image portability** — the symlink approach works if the base image is ever swapped to a non-Debian distribution (Alpine, UBI, etc.). The package is Debian-only.

If maintainers prefer the package on the gateway image specifically, happy to split that out — the sandbox Dockerfiles would still want the conditional symlink pattern for the portability reason.

## Test plan

- [x] Rebuild `openclaw:default` locally; verify `python --version` succeeds inside a running gateway container where it previously errored.
- [x] Rebuild a slim variant without python3; verify the new layer is a no-op and the image still builds.
- [x] Existing agent workflows that reach for `python` stop burning tool round trips.

## AI disclosure

AI-assisted fix, lightly tested (manual rebuild + smoke test across variants; no automated test coverage since the repo doesn't run Dockerfile-level tests). I understand what the code does.
