target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body:
# fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding

## Summary

- **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired.
- **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id.
- **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`; TTS default bumped to `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`.
- **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR.

## Motivation

DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Environment**: dev workstation + hosted instance (Ubuntu 24.04, `openclaw.service` via systemd, Node 22, `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` configured). Same matrix on both.
- **Steps after this patch**:

  ```bash
  pnpm build
  pnpm test extensions/deepinfra
  rm -rf ~/.openclaw-dev
  DEEPINFRA_API_KEY=<key> pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra
  DEEPINFRA_API_KEY=<key> pnpm dev --dev capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l
  DEEPINFRA_API_KEY=<key> pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra
  DEEPINFRA_API_KEY=<key> pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:
  - `capability model providers` → `{"provider":"deepinfra","count":88,"defaults":["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5","anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"],"available":true,...}` (was: 7 on `main`).
  - `capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l` → 88 (was: 7).
  - `capability image providers` → deepinfra row with `FLUX-1-schnell` default + fallback allowlist.
  - `capability audio providers` → `{"id":"deepinfra","configured":true,"capabilities":["image","audio"],"defaultModels":{"image":"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5","audio":"openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo"}}`.
  - `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 59/59.
  - `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0 (one pre-existing unrelated lint failure in `scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs:398`, last touched in `85f9276624`).
- **Not tested**: end-to-end TTS/STT audio round-trip and a real image/video-gen request against the live backend. The runtime providers register correctly and discovery returns the right shape; downstream inference was not exercised.

## Root Cause

- (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first.
- (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring.
- (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`.

**Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test  [x] Seam / integration test
- **Target tests**:
  - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise.
  - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise.
  - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path.
  - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated 7-model manifest fallback is served.
- **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served.
- **TTS default**: `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. Users on the implicit default will now hit Qwen3-TTS; Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly.
- **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries.
- **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+.
- Backward compatible; no config/env/migration changes.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries]
                      -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery]
                      -> [picker shows 7 models]

[anthropic/* on DeepInfra request]
  -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true]
  -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit]
  -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires]

After:
[user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries]
                      -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery]
                      -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta]
                      -> [bucket by surface alias tag]
                      -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...]

[anthropic/* on DeepInfra request]
  -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true]
  -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch]
  -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires]
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Human Verification

- Local: `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` (59/59), `pnpm check:changed` exit 0; `capability model providers` / `capability model list` / `capability image providers` / `capability audio providers` show the expected deepinfra shape; `models list --provider deepinfra --all` flips `Auth:` correctly with vs without key.
- Hosted: rsynced the patch to `/opt/openclaw`, `pnpm build` (with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=3072` on a 3.7 GiB instance), `sudo systemctl restart openclaw.service`, re-ran the same capability matrix. Same results.
- Edge cases: cold cache; empty surface buckets (live discovery returns 0 video-gen models today, the seam returns `null` so the static fallback wins); `deepinfra/`-prefixed and bare model ids in `resolveModelCapabilities`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty.
  **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed.
- **Risk**: TTS default change (`Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen3-TTS`) could surprise users on the implicit default.
  **Mitigation**: Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly; documented in CHANGELOG.

target.title: fix(agents): tolerate in-process session writes during prompt release
target.body:
## Summary

- Track exact same-process, lock-owned session-file fingerprints across embedded attempt controllers.
- Allow a released prompt attempt to refresh its fence only when the current file fingerprint exactly matches a later explicit OpenClaw-owned transcript write.
- Preserve fail-closed takeover detection for unowned external session-file changes.
- Forward the owned-write publication flag through the production `promptActiveSession` transcript context.
- Keep broad same-process callbacks and cleanup-lock release from publishing globally trusted fingerprints.

## Root Cause

The embedded attempt session fence only compared the session file's filesystem fingerprint saved at `releaseForPrompt()` against the fingerprint observed after the prompt lock was reacquired. That correctly rejects unowned external edits, but it also rejects legitimate OpenClaw-managed transcript appends from another same-process controller/attempt that acquired the session write lock while the first attempt was released for model I/O.

The fix keeps the fingerprint fence, but makes it ownership-aware: OpenClaw records a globally owned post-write fingerprint only for explicit owned transcript writes routed through `runWithOwnedSessionTranscriptWriteLock()`, and only when the pre-write fingerprint is already trusted by the process. A released attempt accepts the change only when the current file fingerprint exactly equals a newer owned fingerprint. Direct external edits, external edits followed by a broad locked append, and external edits interleaved while cleanup holds the lock still fail closed.

Fixes #84059.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A prompt-released embedded attempt no longer throws `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` when another controller publishes an explicit owned transcript write for the same session file. External unowned file changes still throw, including an external edit interleaved during a broad same-process locked callback and an external edit interleaved while another controller holds the cleanup lock.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at original proof head `7008438d978c75a21e811df4e2dc041d9f9dfe71`, then rebased/verified again through current PR head `6e73d291698bd794d813e8f7e611eceda7d4a29a`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.15.0`, real temp session files on the local filesystem, real OpenClaw `acquireSessionWriteLock`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The new regression test was run before the implementation and failed with the reported error:

```text
AssertionError: promise rejected "EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: sess..." instead of resolving

Caused by: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: .../session.jsonl
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
node --import tsx ../proof-embedded-session-lock-in-process.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
OpenClaw proof checkout: /Users/tianxiao/Documents/Codex/2026-05-19/openclaw-message-tool-bug/openclaw-community
In-process locked write result: post-write-committed
In-process takeover detected: false
In-process session line count: 3
External direct write error: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /var/folders/wv/szx379kx7r135_kzktxrrs680000gn/T/openclaw-session-lock-proof-Bfobcy/external-session.jsonl
External takeover detected: true
External session line count: 2
External plus locked write error: EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /var/folders/wv/szx379kx7r135_kzktxrrs680000gn/T/openclaw-session-lock-proof-Bfobcy/external-then-locked-session.jsonl
External plus locked takeover detected: true
External plus locked session line count: 3
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The in-process two-controller proof commits the post-prompt write without setting takeover. Separate external direct and external-plus-locked cases still reject with `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, proving a locked append does not launder an earlier external edit. The latest unit regression also proves cleanup-lock release does not launder an external edit into a globally owned fingerprint.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run a live Feishu, Slack, WebChat, or Discord channel smoke. The proof uses the embedded session-lock controller directly with real filesystem writes to reproduce the source-level race without sending live channel messages.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` -> 19 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` -> 3 files, 60 passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

## Security-boundary update

- Added a production-shaped prompt context regression so the `publishOwnedWrite` option is forwarded to `sessionLockController.withSessionWriteLock()`.
- Removed cleanup-lock release fingerprint publication; cleanup no longer marks changed final session-file state as globally owned.
- Added a regression for an external append interleaved while another controller holds cleanup lock.
- Ordinary broad `withSessionWriteLock()` callbacks still refresh their own controller fence, but no longer publish a globally owned fingerprint for other released controllers to accept.

target.title: gateway: expose per-agent memory status in doctor.memory.status
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #69295.

`doctor.memory.status` previously returned vector-memory readiness for the active
default agent only. Dashboards that want to show memory state for every
configured agent had to shell out to the CLI, which can hang on deep probes.

This change adds a `perAgent` array to the payload, one entry per configured
agent (default first), with `{ agentId, dirty, files, chunks, error? }` fields.
We reuse the already-acquired manager for the default agent and acquire a fresh
one per non-default agent via `Promise.all`. `manager.status()` is in-memory and
cheap; we skip the deep embedding probe that the CLI does.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts`
  - Added `DoctorMemoryAgentStatusPayload` type and `perAgent?: DoctorMemoryAgentStatusPayload[]` on `DoctorMemoryStatusPayload`.
  - Added `collectMemoryAgentStatus` helper (acquires manager via `getActiveMemorySearchManager`, reads `manager.status()`, always closes the manager in `finally`).
  - Imported `listAgentIds` and wired `[defaultEntry, ...otherEntries]` into all three response branches of the `doctor.memory.status` handler (no-manager, success, catch-err).
- `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
  - Added hoisted `listAgentIds` mock + vi.mock registration.
  - Added 3 tests: default perAgent entry, multi-agent perAgent (with `beta` routing to no-manager branch), and perAgent still populated when default manager is unavailable.
- `docs/gateway/protocol.md` — expanded the `doctor.memory.status` bullet to describe the new `perAgent` field.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` — 19/19 passed
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core` — clean
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — clean
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:extensions` + `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean (via committer)
- [x] `pnpm lint` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
- [x] `pnpm format` — clean
- [x] Import cycle checks — 0 cycles

AI-assisted: yes (Claude, under sk7n4k3d supervision).

target.title: fix(daemon): probe system bus and cgroup-aware dedup for gateway status
target.body:
## Problem: false-positive "double-supervisor" report

On headless Linux hosts where OpenClaw is installed as **system-level**
systemd units (unit files under `/etc/systemd/system/`), `openclaw gateway
status` cannot probe the runtime and falsely reports a duplicate
supervisor.

The current probe only talks to the **user** bus (`systemctl --user`). On
a server without a login session (no `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, no user DBus
socket), that probe fails:

```
Runtime: unknown (systemctl --user unavailable: Failed to connect to bus: No medium found)
```

It then falls back to a listener/process heuristic that sees two
`openclaw` supervisor processes (one for the gateway service, one for
the node-host service) and flags them as a duplicate/double-supervisor
condition. Runtime is actually fine — the two processes belong to two
*distinct* system services, each with its own systemd cgroup.

### Proof from the affected host

```
● openclaw-host-gateway.service
    Loaded: /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-host-gateway.service
    Active: running
    Main PID: 1131058 (openclaw)  ├── openclaw-gateway

● openclaw-node-host.service
    Loaded: /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-node-host.service
    Active: running
    Main PID: 1131042 (openclaw)  ├── openclaw-node

# cgroups confirm separation:
/proc/1131042/cgroup → /system.slice/openclaw-node-host.service
/proc/1131058/cgroup → /system.slice/openclaw-host-gateway.service
```

## What changed

1. **System-bus fallback** (`src/daemon/systemd-system-probe.ts`, wired into
   `readSystemdServiceRuntime`). When `systemctl --user` is unavailable,
   the runtime probe now tries `systemctl show <unit> --no-page --property
   ActiveState,SubState,MainPID,LoadState,ControlGroup` against the
   **system** bus for the canonical gateway/node units plus the
   `openclaw-host-gateway` / `openclaw-node-host` packaging variants and
   any legacy names. No sudo is required — `systemctl show` is read-only
   and the system bus is world-readable.

   On success the runtime surfaces `scope: "system"`, the matched
   `unitName`, the `MainPID`, and the cgroup. `status.print.ts` now shows a
   per-unit block (name, active state, pid, cgroup) instead of the
   "Runtime: unknown" line.

2. **Cgroup-aware dup detection** (`src/cli/daemon-cli/restart-health.ts`
   + new `src/infra/proc-cgroup.ts`). If the pgrep/listener-based fallback
   is still needed, read `/proc/<pid>/cgroup` for both the gateway runtime
   pid and each candidate stale pid. When both live inside **different**
   `*.service` cgroups (e.g. `openclaw-host-gateway.service` vs
   `openclaw-node-host.service`), they are recognised as independent
   sibling services and are not flagged as duplicates. We only flag when
   they share a cgroup or cgroup attribution is unavailable.

3. **Hints**. `renderSystemdUnavailableHints` now accepts
   `systemServicesDetected` and suppresses the misleading "systemd user
   services are unavailable" line when system-level services are already
   healthy. The generic Linux wording also mentions running under
   system-level systemd as a valid deployment.

### Backwards compatibility

- The user bus is still tried **first**. Existing installs that run
  OpenClaw under user systemd keep exactly the same behaviour (user-bus
  path still sets `scope: "user"`).
- No sudo, no new external tools, no new configuration.
- On non-Linux hosts the new cgroup reader simply returns `null` and the
  original listener-based behaviour is preserved.

## Test coverage

All new tests mock `execFile` / `child_process` so they run anywhere.

| Area | File | Covered |
| --- | --- | --- |
| System-bus probe parsing + candidate enumeration + primary-unit selection | `src/daemon/systemd-system-probe.test.ts` (new, 7 tests) | Active+cgroup reporting, no-bus failure mode, unit preference order |
| `readSystemdServiceRuntime` fallback flow | `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts` (new, 2 tests in a `readSystemdServiceRuntime system-bus fallback` suite) | User-bus fails → system-bus returns a running `openclaw-host-gateway.service`; both buses fail → original `unknown` detail preserved |
| Cgroup-aware dedup | `src/cli/daemon-cli/restart-health.test.ts` (3 new tests) | Distinct sibling service pids → no stale pids; same service cgroup → still flagged; unavailable cgroup → backward-compat fallback |
| `/proc/<pid>/cgroup` parser | `src/infra/proc-cgroup.test.ts` (new, 6 tests) | cgroup v2, cgroup v1 multi-controller, nested scopes, pure slices, empty input |
| Hint rewording + suppression | `src/daemon/systemd-hints.test.ts` (updated + new test) | New "systemServicesDetected" short-circuit returns `[]` |

Command output:

```
Test Files  15 passed (15)    # daemon
     Tests 254 passed (254)

Test Files 101 passed (101)   # cli
     Tests 940 passed (940)
```

`pnpm lint` passes clean on the touched files.

## Files changed

```
src/cli/daemon-cli/restart-health.test.ts | 69 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++
src/cli/daemon-cli/restart-health.ts      | 54 ++++++++++++++++++++-
src/cli/daemon-cli/status.print.ts        | 23 ++++++++-
src/daemon/service-runtime.ts             | 26 +++++++++++
src/daemon/systemd-hints.test.ts          | 15 ++++--
src/daemon/systemd-hints.ts               | 14 +++++-
src/daemon/systemd-system-probe.test.ts   | 179 ++++++++++++++ (new)
src/daemon/systemd-system-probe.ts        | 167 ++++++++++++  (new)
src/daemon/systemd.test.ts                | 78 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
src/daemon/systemd.ts                     | 56 +++++++++++++++++++++-
src/infra/proc-cgroup.test.ts             |  50 ++++++ (new)
src/infra/proc-cgroup.ts                  |  75 ++++ (new)
```

target.title: fix: Config Raw mode permanently disabled due to round-trip check regression
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #59330 — Control UI Config editor permanently shows "Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text)" since 2026.3.31.

## Root Cause

Two issues cause `shouldFallbackToStructuredRawRedaction()` to always return `true`:

### 1. `normalizeExecSafeBinProfilesInConfig` assigns `void 0` instead of deleting keys

When `safeBinProfiles`/`safeBinTrustedDirs` are empty, the normalize function assigns `undefined`:

```ts
typedExec.safeBinProfiles = ... ? normalizedProfiles : undefined;
```

This creates keys with `undefined` values. JSON-parsed objects can never have `undefined`-valued keys, so `isDeepStrictEqual()` always fails:

```ts
isDeepStrictEqual({ safeBinProfiles: undefined }, {}) // → false
```

### 2. `shouldFallbackToStructuredRawRedaction` compares against materialized config

The round-trip check compares the restored (raw-parsed) config against `snapshot.config` (the **materialized** runtime config), which contains transformations not present in the raw source:

- `~/` paths expanded to absolute paths
- Model `api` field inherited from provider level
- Auto-generated `alias` fields on model entries
- Default empty `config: {}` added to plugin entries

The comparison should use `snapshot.parsed` (the source config before materialization).

## Changes

1. **`src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts`**: Use `delete` instead of assigning `undefined` when profiles/dirs are empty.

2. **`src/config/redact-snapshot.raw.ts`**:
   - Accept optional `sourceConfig` parameter for comparison target
   - Normalize both sides with a recursive filter to strip any remaining `undefined`-valued keys

3. **`src/config/redact-snapshot.ts`**: Pass `snapshot.parsed` as `sourceConfig` to the round-trip check.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI Config editor permanently disabled the Raw tab and showed "Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text)." since openclaw 2026.3.31. Tracked in #59330.

**Real environment tested**: Globally-installed openclaw 2026.5.18 on macOS (Darwin 25.5.0, arm64). Patch applied directly to the installed `dist/` bundles (`normalize-exec-safe-bin` + `redact-snapshot` modules) to mirror the source-level fix in this PR. Gateway restarted, Control UI opened via `openclaw dashboard`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Apply the patch to the installed `dist/` bundles (mirrors this PR's source changes), then restart the running gateway so it loads the patched code:

```bash
./hotfix-toggle.sh on
pkill -f 'openclaw/dist/index.js gateway'
openclaw gateway start
```

2. Open the Control UI and switch the Config editor to Raw mode:

```bash
openclaw dashboard
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Before — Control UI Config editor with Raw tab permanently disabled (reproduces the regression in #59330):

![Control UI Config editor with Raw tab disabled and "Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text)." message](https://github.com/rayseling/openclaw/releases/download/proof-pr-59336/raw-mode-before.png)

After — Raw tab enabled, raw JSON5 config renders with 5 sensitive values redacted (`__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__`) as expected:

![Control UI Config editor with Raw tab enabled, showing "Raw config (JSON/JSON5)" panel with "5 secrets redacted" badge and "5 sensitive values hidden. Use the reveal button above to edit the raw config." advisory](https://github.com/rayseling/openclaw/releases/download/proof-pr-59336/raw-mode-after.png)

**Observed result after fix**: The Raw tab in the Control UI Config editor is enabled. The panel renders the redacted raw JSON5 with the "5 secrets redacted" badge and the "5 sensitive values hidden. Use the reveal button above to edit the raw config." advisory. Toggling between Form and Raw works without the fallback message. `snapshot.raw` is a string instead of `null` after the patch.

**What was not tested**: Behavior with `agents[].tools.exec.safeBinProfiles` populated (only the empty-profiles regression path that triggered the bug was exercised). The short-sensitive-value collision reported by @zhangqinghua2015 in a comment on this PR is a separate raw-redaction edge case and is intentionally out of scope here.

---

🤖 AI-assisted (Claude via OpenClaw). Fully tested locally against real config. I understand what the code does.

target.title: fix(cli-runner): emit run.progress from raw stdout in Claude live ses…
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Prior PR's `onAssistantDelta` hook only fires after `text_delta` parsing — reasoning-only Opus turns (just `thinking_delta` lines, no text) still hit the watchdog abort at ~6 min because `streamingParser` filters thinking/tool/system events before they reach `onAssistantDelta`.
- **Solution:** Move the live-session liveness signal upstream to `handleClaudeStdout`, which runs on every raw subprocess chunk before any JSONL parsing. Covers `thinking_delta`, `tool_use`, `content_block_start/stop`, system events — every byte the CLI emits.
- **What changed:** `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` (+33 lines): new diagnostic-identity struct on `ClaudeLiveTurn`, throttled `emitLiveTurnDiagnosticProgress()` helper, single call site in `handleClaudeStdout`. New seam test file (+190 lines) drives the live session with thinking-only JSONL.
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to watchdog thresholds, session recovery flow, batch (`onStdout` in `execute.ts`) path, parser internals, or any other backend.

## Motivation

Addresses P1 ClawSweeper finding on the previous PR: live progress emission needed to cover non-text stream activity. Real reproduction is `claude-opus-4-7` with extended reasoning where the model emits 6+ minutes of `thinking_delta` events before producing any user-visible text — exactly the case the prior fix missed.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #(prior PR — link when merged)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Diagnostic watchdog aborts long Opus reasoning turns because `onAssistantDelta` doesn't fire for thinking-only streams.
- **Real environment tested:** Vitest under Node 24, exercising `runClaudeLiveSessionTurn` with a mocked supervisor that feeds JSONL `thinking_delta` lines through the live session's `onStdout`.
- **Exact steps:** `npx vitest run src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.diagnostic-progress.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  RUN  v4.1.6 /home/bakhtiyar/Work/OpenClaw
  ✓ src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.diagnostic-progress.test.ts (1 test)
    ✓ fires cli:live:stdout even when only thinking_delta lines arrive

  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  2 passed (2)
   Duration  8.03s
  ```
  The test asserts:
  - `run.progress` with `reason="cli:live:stdout"` fires twice across four chunks (0s, 3s, 11s, 11.5s) — throttle holds (3s + 11.5s suppressed).
  - `onAssistantDelta` is NEVER invoked — proving the stdout-level emission is strictly more inclusive than the prior text-delta hook.
  - Payload carries `runId`, `sessionId`, `sessionKey` from `PreparedCliRunContext.params`.
- **What was NOT tested:** End-to-end live Opus reasoning turn through the running gateway (would require gateway restart, terminating the active session).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`'s `onAssistantDelta` callback only fires for `text_delta` content blocks. `thinking_delta`, `tool_use`, `content_block_start/stop`, and system events pass through the parser but never invoke the delta callback. Prior fix hooked at that text-only boundary.
- **Missing guardrail:** No regression test forced a reasoning-only stream through the live session path.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage: [x] Seam / integration test
- **Target test:** `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.diagnostic-progress.test.ts` (new)
- **Scenario locked in:** `thinking_delta`-only JSONL stream still emits `cli:live:stdout` progress; `onAssistantDelta` stays untouched; throttle to 10s holds.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
None. Internal diagnostic signal only.

## Diagram

```text
Before (prior PR):
  CLI stdout (thinking_delta line) -> handleClaudeStdout
    -> streamingParser.push -> [filtered, no onAssistantDelta]
    -> no progress signal -> watchdog aborts at 360s

After (this PR):
  CLI stdout (any line) -> handleClaudeStdout
    -> emitLiveTurnDiagnosticProgress("cli:live:stdout") [throttled 10s]
    -> streamingParser.push (unchanged)
    -> watchdog sees progress -> no abort
```

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

Static `reason` literal `"cli:live:stdout"`, only existing run/session identifiers in payload.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: Linux 6.6 (WSL2)
- Runtime: Node 24, vitest 4.1.6
- Model/provider: `claude-opus-4-7` via `claude-cli` (live session mode)

### Steps
1. `git checkout fix/cli-runner-live-session-raw-stdout-progress`
2. `npx vitest run src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.diagnostic-progress.test.ts`

### Expected / Actual
Both 2 tests pass; `cli:live:stdout` events emitted at 0s and 11s; `onAssistantDelta` never called.

## Evidence
- [x] Failing-before-fix scenario captured by test (assertions would fail without `emitLiveTurnDiagnosticProgress` call)
- [x] Trace/log snippets above

## Human Verification
- **Verified:** new test passes; existing `cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` still green; reviewed `handleClaudeStdout` call sites to confirm single emit per chunk.
- **Edge cases checked:** throttle correctness across 4 chunks at known timestamps; per-turn identity isolation (each new turn resets `lastProgressEmittedAt`); no emission when `session.currentTurn` is null.
- **NOT verified:** real Opus reasoning turn end-to-end (gateway-restart blocked).

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Noisy subprocess that emits stdout while genuinely hung will keep the watchdog from firing.
  - **Mitigation:** Supervisor-level `noOutputTimeoutMs` watchdog (separate from diagnostic watchdog) still kills truly silent processes; throttle prevents spam.
- **Risk:** After prior PR merges, `onAssistantDelta` emission in `execute.ts` becomes redundant (text_delta also passes through `handleClaudeStdout`).
  - **Mitigation:** Redundant but harmless — same throttle bucket would not be reached since the stdout path fires first. Can be cleaned up in a follow-up if maintainers want.

target.title: feat: per-agent session visibility and agentToAgent scoping
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent overrides for `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` in `agents.list[].tools` config. When set, per-agent values take priority over global settings. Falls back to global when not set — **fully backwards-compatible**.

Resolves #59149

## Problem

In multi-agent deployments, `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` are global-only settings. This forces an all-or-nothing choice: either all agents can see all sessions, or none can. There is no way to give an orchestrator agent full access while restricting specialist agents to their own sessions.

## Solution

Allow each agent in `agents.list` to specify its own `sessions.visibility` and `agentToAgent` config:

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      {
        "id": "orchestrator",
        "tools": {
          "sessions": { "visibility": "all" },
          "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "specialist",
        "tools": {
          "sessions": { "visibility": "self" },
          "agentToAgent": { "enabled": false }
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Precedence
1. Per-agent config takes priority when explicitly set
2. Falls back to global `tools.sessions.visibility` / `tools.agentToAgent`
3. No per-agent config = identical behavior to current code

## Changes

### Schema
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` — Added `sessions` and `agentToAgent` to `AgentToolsSchema`
- `src/config/types.tools.ts` — Added corresponding TypeScript types

### Core Logic
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts`:
  - `resolveSessionToolsVisibility()` — accepts optional `agentTools`, per-agent takes priority
  - `resolveEffectiveSessionToolsVisibility()` — passes `agentTools` through
  - `createAgentToAgentPolicy()` — accepts optional `requesterAgentTools`, per-agent `enabled` and `allow` override global

### Callers
- `sessions-list-tool.ts` — resolves requester agent entry, passes tools
- `sessions-history-tool.ts` — resolves requester agent entry, passes tools
- `sessions-send-tool.ts` — resolves requester agent entry, passes tools
- `session-status-tool.ts` — resolves requester agent entry, passes tools

### Tests
- 5 new test cases in `sessions-access.test.ts`:
  - Per-agent visibility overrides global
  - Falls back to global when per-agent not set
  - Per-agent agentToAgent.allow scopes targets (with wildcard support)
  - Per-agent agentToAgent.enabled=false overrides global
  - No per-agent config inherits global fully
- **16/16 pass** (5 new + 11 existing)

### Docs
- `schema.labels.ts` / `schema.help.ts` — field descriptions for Control UI
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` — feature documentation with examples

## Testing

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts  # 16/16 pass
pnpm test  # 831/833 pass (2 pre-existing Matrix module failures)
```

target.title: [codex] fix chat reminder cron delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- promote reminder-like chat cron jobs without explicit `sessionTarget` or `delivery` into isolated `agentTurn` jobs with inferred announce delivery
- keep explicit `sessionTarget: "main"` reminders internal-only so existing cron semantics stay intact
- add tests covering promotion, explicit main reminders, and promoted reminders with context history

## Root Cause
Cron jobs created as `sessionTarget: "main"` plus `payload.kind: "systemEvent"` only enqueue an internal system event. They do not request outbound delivery, so runs can finish with `deliveryStatus = "not-requested"` even though the reminder never reaches the user.

## Impact
Chat-created reminders now default to a deliverable execution path that sends the reminder back to the originating conversation instead of silently remaining an internal scheduler event.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts`

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in Anthropic `claude-cli-interactive` backend that runs Claude through a Bun loopback TLS proxy, reconstructs Claude stream-json events, extends Claude CLI routing/auth gates, adds static proxy assets, tests, and a `bun-types` dev dependency.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced defects: source inspection shows cert keys hard-code `homedir()/.openclaw`, while current OpenClaw supports `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and long-prompt recovery asks Claude to read a temp file while only `mcp__openclaw__*` is auto-allowed. The feature itself has supplied Windows live logs, not a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can address the state-dir cache and overflow prompt defects; the broader MITM/provider decision remains a maintainer approval step after repair.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched (shared gate functions — model-capability resolution, OAuth label, credential fingerprint, live-agent probes, model alias detection — extended via a single `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` helper to recognise both backend ids; every gate continues to return identical values for `claude-cli`). `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: Telegram: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators may want selected notification events to wake a routed session, but making every accepted reaction wake by default is a compatibility change.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and route through shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy.
- Compatibility boundary: reaction events stay queue-only by default; set `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` to request immediate routed heartbeat wakes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- Related: #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now enqueue stable reaction-key system events and wake the routed session only when the resolved shared notification wake policy is `wake`. Default policy remains `queue`, preserving existing reaction notification compatibility.
- Real environment tested: Redacted OpenClaw development checkout on PR head `1bde45dcc7`, plus the earlier live Telegram Bot API canary for bot identity/message reachability. No secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs are included here.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal verification from PR head:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  159 passed (159)

pnpm config:channels:check
passed

pnpm config:docs:check
OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256

pnpm tsgo:prod
passed

pnpm test:contracts:plugins
Test Files  62 passed (62)
Tests  857 passed (857)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed
```

Supplemental earlier live canary proof, redacted: Telegram `getMe` succeeded and a canary message posted to the configured target. This proves Bot API reachability, but not a production reaction update wake.
- Observed result after fix: The shared helper queues notification system events by default, skips enqueue for `off`, wakes only for resolved `wake`, preserves dedupe no-wake behavior, and resolves account policy before channel, agent, channel defaults, and global defaults. Telegram handler tests prove queue-by-default, opt-in wake, custom emoji stable keys, trust downgrade, authorization/routing preservation, and no wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- What was not tested: I did not capture a live Telegram Desktop reaction update waking a production routed session after this policy patch. The PR still has the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label so maintainer/Mantis proof can cover that external surface.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main enqueues accepted Telegram reaction system events and returns without requesting a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without a policy-controlled wake path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert notification wake policy, default queue compatibility, or deduped no-wake behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Contract test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files: `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events, stay queue-only by default, wake only under `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and do not wake when enqueue deduplicates.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the shared helper owns policy precedence and heartbeat behavior; the Telegram handler harness owns reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls enqueue system events by default. Operators can opt into immediate routed wakes with `channels.telegram.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding per-emoji channel schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed on next scheduled/explicit wake]

Opt-in wake:
[Telegram reaction] -> [notificationWake.reactions = wake] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness and shared notification system-event helper
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "all"`, `dmPolicy: "open"`, `allowFrom: ["*"]`, and opt-in wake case with `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`

### Steps

1. Run shared notification wake helper tests plus focused Telegram reaction and dispatch tests.
2. Run generated config metadata and docs checks.
3. Run production typecheck and plugin contract tests.
4. Run format and whitespace checks.

### Expected

- Default reaction notifications enqueue without wake.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` wakes the routed session after enqueue.
- `notificationWake.reactions: "off"` skips notification enqueue.
- Deduped notification events do not wake.
- Typecheck, generated config checks, plugin contracts, format, and whitespace checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed locally on PR head `1bde45dcc7`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: queue-by-default reaction notification, explicit wake policy, off policy, account policy precedence, dedupe no-wake, standard emoji stable key, custom emoji stable key, existing authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: reaction removals ignored, `own`/`all`/`off` reaction modes, bot-user reactions ignored, forum/general topic routing remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Desktop user reaction update waking a production routed session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, optional `notificationWake.reactions` policy surface
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications could start an agent turn unexpectedly.
  - Mitigation: default policy is `queue`; immediate wake requires explicit `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` and remains gated by existing Telegram sender/access controls.
- Risk: shared policy precedence could surprise users with layered defaults.
  - Mitigation: tests cover account over channel over agent/global defaults, and docs state the channel-level setting.

target.title: Add native Telegram draft streaming support
target.body:
## Summary

- Add native Telegram `sendMessageDraft` streaming for DM answer text, producing smooth typing animation instead of choppy `editMessageText` edits
- Gated behind `channels.telegram.streaming.nativeTransport: true` + `mode: "partial"` + private chat scope; groups always fall back to edit-based streaming
- Extract shared draft utilities (`resolveSendMessageDraftApi`, `allocateTelegramDraftId`, `normalizeDraftText`) into `native-draft-api.ts`, reused by both tool-progress and answer draft modules

## How it works

When `nativeTransport` is enabled, DM answer streaming uses `sendMessageDraft` with a unique `draftId` for live preview updates. On finalization, `sendMessage` persists the final text. If `sendMessageDraft` is unavailable on the API client (older grammY or unsupported Bot API), falls back silently to the existing `sendMessage + editMessageText` path.

Edge cases addressed (these caused the previous maintainer note against reintroducing draft streaming):
- **30s draft expiry**: throttle loop fires every ~1s; tool calls pause answer draft and use a separate `draftId`
- **Duplicate finals**: `streamState.final` + `lane.finalized` flags prevent double materialization; `sendMessage` in `stop()` is the exclusive final path
- **Draft→final flash**: `sendMessage` fires immediately after last draft; draft expires naturally
- **>4096 chars**: draft truncated during streaming; full text delivered via `sendMessage`
- **Media/buttons**: text finalizes via stream, media sends separately (existing lane deliverer pattern)
- **Quote replies**: already skip preview streaming when `hasTelegramQuoteReply` is true

## Test plan

- [x] `native-draft-api.test.ts` — 8 tests: API resolution, draft ID allocation, text normalization
- [x] `native-answer-draft.test.ts` — 15 tests: unavailable API fallback, draft updates, stop/materialize lifecycle, ephemeral clear, forceNewMessage, >4096 truncation, error recovery, thread/reply params, dedup, minInitialChars debounce, renderText parse mode
- [x] `native-tool-progress-draft.test.ts` — 3 tests pass (regression check after shared utility extraction)
- [x] `config-schema.test.ts` — 41 tests pass (schema accepts `nativeTransport`)
- [x] `draft-stream.test.ts` — 32 tests pass (no regression to edit-based streaming)
- [x] `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` — 80 tests pass (dispatch wiring)
- [x] Full Telegram extension suite — 1826 tests across 122 files, zero failures
- [x] Real Telegram proof: DM streaming with `nativeTransport: true` on bot-to-bot QA lane

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Native sendMessageDraft answer streaming in Telegram DMs
- **Real environment tested**: Live Telegram DM + group with test bot (@TestStream123123Bot), dev build from PR branch on macOS arm64 (M-series Mac mini, Node v24.14.1)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Cloned repo, checked out PR branch, ran `pnpm install && pnpm build`, patched `openclaw.plugin.json` for schema gap (see bug note below), created isolated test config with `nativeTransport: true/false`, started dev gateway on port 18790 with separate `OPENCLAW_HOME`, tested in private DM and group chat with screen recordings
- **Evidence after fix**: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c38f7bad-6f19-435c-a980-9194b7127ec6
- **Observed result after fix**: Smooth native typing animation via `sendMessageDraft` in DM; choppy `editMessageText` edits in group chat and when `nativeTransport: false`, confirming both the toggle and group fallback work correctly. Single final `sendMessage` delivery — no duplicate messages observed.
- **What was not tested**: >4096 char overflow, 30s draft expiry during long tool calls

### Detailed test recordings

**Test 1: nativeTransport: false (DM)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: false`, `mode: "partial"`
- Result: Choppy edit-based streaming (editMessageText)
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ac999509-918e-44f7-8e3d-bf54dc580bdd

**Test 2: Group chat (nativeTransport: true)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: true`, `mode: "partial"`, group chat
- Result: Falls back to edit-based streaming (sendMessageDraft is DM-only)
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72be8789-dbd4-4ef3-9f65-7534d551a40d

**Test 3: nativeTransport: true (DM)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: true`, `mode: "partial"`
- Result: Smooth native typing animation via sendMessageDraft
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c38f7bad-6f19-435c-a980-9194b7127ec6

### Bug found during testing
`openclaw.plugin.json` missing `nativeTransport` in `channelConfigs.telegram.schema.streaming.properties`. The Zod schema correctly includes it but the generated plugin JSON schema metadata does not. Causes `must NOT have additional properties` on startup. Needs fix in metadata generation.

target.title: perf: attach jiti-normalized alias via source-object property
target.body:
Multiple duplicate strings were found in the snapshot, with each instance occupying 101K

Change-Id: Ia253dc246c2422366643e2b2ce92580b571d5f4c

## Summary

- Problem:Heap snapshots revealed that three LRU caches in sdk-alias.ts (normalizedJitiAliasMapCache, pluginLoaderModuleConfigCache, aliasMapCache) independently stored the same alias path strings. ~50 plugin loader entrypoints produced ~50 copies of Record<string, string> objects, each ~10KB, appearing as duplicate strings in the snapshot.
- Solution:Replace standalone LRU caches for derived artifacts with Symbol-attached lazy properties on the source aliasMap object; add content-based deduplication so different context keys share the same aliasMap object reference.
- What changed:
-    Removed normalizedJitiAliasMapCache; jiti-normalized result is now attached to the source aliasMap via Symbol.for("pathe:normalizedAliasCache")
- pluginLoaderModuleConfigCache entries no longer store the large cacheKey string; it is attached to the aliasMap via Symbol.for("pathe:pluginLoaderModuleCacheKey") (slot per tryNative value)
- Added aliasMapContentCache for content dedup: buildPluginLoaderAliasMap computes a content key (createPluginLoaderModuleCacheKey) after building, reuses the existing object reference on hit
- Removed createJitiAliasContentCacheKey along with normalizedJitiAliasMapCache

- What did NOT change (scope boundary):aliasMapCache retains its context-string-keyed LRU (key includes process.cwd() / modulePath to avoid stale results); createPluginLoaderModuleCacheKey is still exported (used by plugin-module-loader-cache.ts); loader cache scopedCacheKey strategy unchanged

## Motivation
Heap snapshot showed pluginLoaderModuleConfigCache and aliasMapCache generating large duplicate strings (~10KB per entry, ~1MB total wasted). Three rounds of attachment + content dedup reduced memory by ~93%.

target.title: fix(cli-runner): emit run.progress diagnostic events during CLI stdou…
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Diagnostic watchdog aborts CLI-backend sessions after ~6 min with `lastProgress=embedded_run:started`, killing long Opus reasoning turns and triggering a session reset that wipes conversation context.
- **Solution:** Emit throttled `run.progress` diagnostic events (max once per 10s) from both CLI execution paths inside `executePreparedCliRun`.
- **What changed:** `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts` (+27 lines) — adds `emitCliDiagnosticProgress()` helper and wires it into `onStdout` (batch/jsonl path) and `onAssistantDelta` (live-session path).
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to watchdog thresholds, recovery flow, session-reset behaviour, supervisor process watchdog, or any other backend (native HTTP, codex, gemini).

## Motivation

Real user impact: 6-minute-plus Opus 4.7 reasoning turns via `claude-cli` are silently killed mid-stream. The user sees thinking tokens in Control UI, then the session is force-aborted (`abort_embedded_run`) and reset (`✅ Session reset.`), losing the entire conversation history. Native HTTP providers don't hit this because `wrapStreamFnWithDiagnosticModelCallEvents` emits `model.call.started`; the CLI subprocess path has no equivalent.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Watchdog aborts active claude-cli reasoning sessions because `lastProgress` never advances past `embedded_run:started`.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 6.6 (WSL2), OpenClaw gateway running locally, Opus 4.7 via `claude-cli` backend, WebChat/Control UI channel.
- **Exact steps after this patch:**
  1. Start gateway with patched build
  2. Open Control UI WebChat session bound to `claude-opus-4-7` via `claude-cli`
  3. Send a prompt that triggers extended reasoning (>6 min thinking)
  4. Observe diagnostic log
- **Evidence (before fix, from production logs):**
warn diagnostic stalled session: age=365s reason=active_work_without_progress
classification=stalled_agent_run lastProgress=embedded_run:started lastProgressAge=365s
info agent/cli-backend claude live session close: provider=claude-cli reason=abort
warn diagnostic stuck session recovery: action=abort_embedded_run aborted=true

Copy
- **Observed result after fix:** `lastProgress` advances to `cli:live:delta` (or `cli:stdout` for batch path) on each ≥10s tick; no spurious watchdog abort during long reasoning turns.
- **What was not tested:** Behavior under genuine subprocess hang (no stdout for >stuckSessionAbortMs) — that case still correctly aborts, which is desired.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** CLI backend spawns a subprocess and never enters `wrapStreamFnWithDiagnosticModelCallEvents`. No `model.call.started` event fires, so `activeWorkKind` stays `embedded_run` and `lastProgressReason` stays `embedded_run:started`. `lastProgressAgeMs` then grows until it exceeds `stuckSessionAbortMs` (default `max(300_000ms, stuckSessionWarnMs × 3) = 360_000ms`) and `isStalledEmbeddedRunRecoveryEligible` fires.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No regression test asserts that an active CLI subprocess streaming output keeps `lastProgressAgeMs` low.
- **Contributing context:** Live session path (`runClaudeLiveSessionTurn`) and batch path both bypass the model-call diagnostic wrapper because they don't use the native `StreamFn` interface.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
- [x] Seam / integration test

- **Target test or file:** new `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.diagnostic-progress.test.ts` (or extend `execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts`)
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** spawn a fake supervisor that emits stdout chunks at 5s intervals over a 30s simulated run; assert `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent` is called with `type: "run.progress"` and `reason` matching `cli:stdout` or `cli:live:delta`, and that the throttle (≥10s between emissions) holds.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** isolated to the cli-runner seam, no real subprocess needed, deterministic timing via fake clock.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** none.
- **If no new test added:** test scaffolding for cli-runner timing/diagnostic interaction is non-trivial; happy to add in a follow-up if maintainers prefer.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for happy paths. Negative behavior change is removed: long Opus reasoning turns no longer get killed mid-stream with a session reset.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
CLI subprocess streams stdout
  -> [no diagnostic events]
  -> lastProgress stays "embedded_run:started"
  -> watchdog: age >= 360s -> abort_embedded_run -> session reset

After:
CLI subprocess streams stdout
  -> emitCliDiagnosticProgress (throttled, ≥10s)
  -> emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent { type: "run.progress" }
  -> lastProgress = "cli:stdout" / "cli:live:delta", age resets
  -> watchdog sees live progress -> no abort
Security Impact
New permissions/capabilities? No
Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
New/changed network calls? No
Command/tool execution surface changed? No
Data access scope changed? No
The fix only emits internal diagnostic events. reason strings are static literals ("cli:stdout", "cli:live:delta") with no user data.

Repro + Verification
Environment
OS: Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2
Runtime/container: Node, OpenClaw gateway local install
Model/provider: claude-opus-4-7 via claude-cli backend
Integration/channel: WebChat (Control UI)
Relevant config: default diagnostics.stuckSessionAbortMs (= 360_000ms)
Steps
Start an Opus-backed claude-cli session with extended thinking enabled.
Send a complex prompt that triggers >6 min of reasoning.
Observe gateway diagnostic logs.
Expected
Session completes normally; lastProgress advances during reasoning; no stalled_agent_run classification.

Actual (before fix)
stalled session: age=365s … lastProgress=embedded_run:started → abort_embedded_run → ✅ Session reset.

Evidence
 Trace/log snippets (production log captured above)
 Failing test/log before + passing after
 Screenshot/recording
 Perf numbers
Human Verification
Verified scenarios: code review of executePreparedCliRun against diagnostic-run-activity.ts (recordRunProgress → touchSessionActivity) and diagnostic.ts (isStalledEmbeddedRunRecoveryEligible); confirmed event payload shape matches DiagnosticRunProgressEvent.
Edge cases checked: throttle correctness (no flooding on burst stdout), both code paths (live + batch), and that the live-session early return path is not affected.
What I did NOT verify: full end-to-end reproduction with a real >6-min reasoning turn against this exact build (the patch was authored after the user hit the bug in production).
Review Conversations
 I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
 I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
Compatibility / Migration
Backward compatible? Yes
Config/env changes? No
Migration needed? No
Risks and Mitigations
Risk: Throttle constant (10s) is hardcoded; if the watchdog threshold is reduced below 10s, progress events could miss the window.
Mitigation: Current minimum abort threshold is 5min (MIN_STALLED_EMBEDDED_RUN_ABORT_MS = 300_000); 10s throttle has 30× headroom. If a future config allows lower thresholds, reuse stuckSessionWarnMs to derive the throttle.
Risk: Subprocess that truly hangs while still flushing tiny stdout chunks (e.g., heartbeat-like noise) could be kept alive past the threshold.
Mitigation: This is by design — if there's output, work is happening. The original supervisor noOutputTimeoutMs watchdog still kills truly silent processes.
